content.text: library organises Stable Diffusion's components into task-specific pipeline classes. Each pipeline bundles the text encoder, VAE, U-Net, scheduler, and optional safety checker into a single object with a callable interface. The components are individually loadable, swappable, and replaceable — you can mix a SDXL U-Net with a custom VAE, or swap the scheduler at runtime without reloading the model weights. Understanding the pipeline abstraction, how components compose, and which optimisations apply where is the foundation for production-quality image generation.
, device placement, and component introspection. Task-specific subclasses handle the concrete denoising loop, input preprocessing, and output postprocessing for each use case.

attrs.alt: Diffusers pipeline class hierarchy: DiffusionPipeline base → StableDiffusionPipeline, SDXL, Flux branches → Img2Img, Inpaint, ControlNet specialisations, with shared swappable components (text encoder, VAE, U-Net, scheduler)

content.text:
from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline, DiffusionPipeline
import torch

# Standard load — downloads and caches all components
pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,   # halve VRAM usage
    use_safetensors=True,        # safer weight format
).to("cuda")

# Inspect what components were loaded
print(type(pipe.text_encoder))    # CLIPTextModel
print(type(pipe.vae))             # AutoencoderKL
print(type(pipe.unet))            # UNet2DConditionModel
print(type(pipe.scheduler))       # PNDMScheduler (default)
print(type(pipe.safety_checker))  # StableDiffusionSafetyChecker

# Access config that was used
print(pipe.vae.config.scaling_factor)   # 0.18215
print(pipe.unet.config.in_channels)     # 4 (latent channels)
print(pipe.unet.config.cross_attention_dim)  # 768 (CLIP hidden dim)

# Check total parameter count
total = sum(p.numel() for p in pipe.unet.parameters())
print(f"U-Net params: {total/1e9:.2f}B")  # ~0.86B for SD 1.5
generates images from text prompts. Its
method accepts prompt strings, a guidance scale, step count, image dimensions, and an optional

import torch
from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline

pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")

# Single image
result = pipe(
    prompt="a macro photograph of a dew drop on a spider web",
    negative_prompt="blurry, low quality, watermark",
    width=512,
    height=512,
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    num_inference_steps=30,
    num_images_per_prompt=1,
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(42),
)
image = result.images[0]
print(result.nsfw_content_detected)   # [False]

# Batch generation — sends all prompts in a single batched forward pass
prompts = [
    "a red fox in a snowy forest",
    "a lighthouse at sunset, oil painting style",
    "a portrait of an astronaut, studio lighting",
]
batch_result = pipe(
    prompt=prompts,
    negative_prompt=["low quality"] * 3,
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    num_inference_steps=25,
)
for i, img in enumerate(batch_result.images):
    img.save(f"output_{i}.png")
encodes an input image into the latent space, adds noise to a partial timestep determined by the
parameter, and runs denoising only for the remaining steps. At
the input image is fully replaced; at

import torch
from diffusers import StableDiffusionImg2ImgPipeline
from PIL import Image

pipe = StableDiffusionImg2ImgPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")

init = Image.open("sketch.png").convert("RGB").resize((512, 512))

result = pipe(
    prompt="a detailed architectural render of a house, photorealistic",
    image=init,
    strength=0.8,          # 80% of steps replace structure; 20% preserve input
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    num_inference_steps=30,
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(0),
).images[0]

# Strength calibration guide:
# 0.1 – 0.3  : subtle retouching, colour grading, texture change
# 0.4 – 0.6  : style transfer while preserving structure
# 0.7 – 0.9  : heavy edit, loosely based on input
# 1.0        : full text-to-image (input image ignored)

The inpainting pipeline requires an image and a binary mask. White (255) pixels in the mask indicate the region to regenerate; black (0) pixels are preserved from the original. The pipeline concatenates the mask and the masked image as extra U-Net input channels — inpainting-specific models such as
are trained with this 9-channel input explicitly.

import torch
from diffusers import StableDiffusionInpaintPipeline
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw

pipe = StableDiffusionInpaintPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-inpainting",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")

image = Image.open("street.png").convert("RGB").resize((512, 512))

# Create a mask programmatically — white region will be regenerated
mask = Image.new("RGB", (512, 512), (0, 0, 0))   # all black (preserve)
draw = ImageDraw.Draw(mask)
draw.ellipse([180, 180, 340, 340], fill=(255, 255, 255))  # white circle = regenerate

result = pipe(
    prompt="a decorative fountain, photorealistic, detailed stone carving",
    negative_prompt="blurry, low quality",
    image=image,
    mask_image=mask,
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    num_inference_steps=50,          # inpainting benefits from more steps
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(5),
).images[0]

# The unmasked region will be pixel-perfect identical to the input
# The masked region is generated fresh, blended at the boundary

SDXL uses a two-stage architecture: a base model that generates a 1024×1024 latent at a low-frequency quality level, optionally followed by a refiner model that sharpens the high-frequency details. The refiner is essentially an img2img pipeline that takes the base output at partial noise and polishes it. The two models can run sequentially, or the base can generate an initial denoised latent that is handed off mid-chain.

import torch
from diffusers import StableDiffusionXLPipeline, StableDiffusionXLImg2ImgPipeline

# Load base model
base = StableDiffusionXLPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
    use_safetensors=True,
).to("cuda")

# Load refiner — shares VAE and text encoder with the base
refiner = StableDiffusionXLImg2ImgPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-refiner-1.0",
    text_encoder_2=base.text_encoder_2,  # reuse to save VRAM
    vae=base.vae,
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
    use_safetensors=True,
).to("cuda")

prompt = "a detailed botanical illustration of a lotus flower, ink and watercolour"
neg    = "blurry, low quality, jpeg artifacts"

# Stage 1: base generates for 80% of steps, outputs a denoised latent
latent = base(
    prompt=prompt,
    negative_prompt=neg,
    output_type="latent",    # return latent, not decoded image
    num_inference_steps=40,
    denoising_end=0.8,       # hand off at t = 0.8 * T
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(3),
).images                     # returns raw latent tensor when output_type="latent"

# Stage 2: refiner runs the remaining 20% of steps on the latent
image = refiner(
    prompt=prompt,
    negative_prompt=neg,
    image=latent,
    num_inference_steps=40,
    denoising_start=0.8,     # picks up where base left off
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(3),
).images[0]

The scheduler is the only pipeline component that can be safely swapped without any model reloading. Different schedulers trade step count against quality: DDPM requires 1000 steps, DDIM needs 50, DPM++ 2M Karras can produce good results in 20. All compatible schedulers can be swapped in via

from diffusers import (
    StableDiffusionPipeline,
    DDIMScheduler,
    DPMSolverMultistepScheduler,
    EulerDiscreteScheduler,
    EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler,
)
import torch

pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5", torch_dtype=torch.float16
).to("cuda")

# Compare schedulers on the same seed
prompt = "a serene Japanese garden, cherry blossoms, koi pond"
gen    = lambda: torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(7)

schedulers = {
    "DDIM-50":     (DDIMScheduler.from_config(pipe.scheduler.config), 50),
    "DPM++2M-20":  (DPMSolverMultistepScheduler.from_config(
                        pipe.scheduler.config,
                        algorithm_type="dpmsolver++",
                        use_karras_sigmas=True,
                    ), 20),
    "Euler-30":    (EulerDiscreteScheduler.from_config(pipe.scheduler.config), 30),
    "EulerA-30":   (EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler.from_config(pipe.scheduler.config), 30),
}

for name, (scheduler, steps) in schedulers.items():
    pipe.scheduler = scheduler
    img = pipe(prompt=prompt, num_inference_steps=steps, generator=gen()).images[0]
    img.save(f"{name}.png")
    print(f"{name}: done")

# DPM++2M Karras at 20 steps is generally the best quality/speed trade-off
# EulerA introduces stochastic variation — different seeds give very different results
The pipeline exposes several memory optimisations that reduce peak VRAM usage at modest speed cost. They are composable — apply multiple to stay within VRAM limits on consumer hardware.

from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline
import torch

# Low-VRAM setup for a 6GB GPU
pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
    safety_checker=None,
    requires_safety_checker=False,
)
# Note: do NOT call .to("cuda") when using model_cpu_offload
# The offload mechanism manages device placement internally
pipe.enable_model_cpu_offload()      # offloads unused submodels to CPU between steps
pipe.enable_attention_slicing(1)     # slice attention to 1 head per step
pipe.enable_vae_slicing()            # decode one image at a time from a batch

image = pipe(
    prompt="a sunset over the ocean, dramatic clouds, cinematic",
    num_inference_steps=25,
    guidance_scale=7.5,
).images[0]

# For 8GB+ GPUs: skip cpu_offload, use only attention_slicing
# For 12GB+:     skip both, optionally use xformers attention
# pipe.enable_xformers_memory_efficient_attention()

parameter accepts a function called at each denoising step with the current step index, timestep, and latent tensor. This enables real-time preview generation, early stopping, and latent manipulation mid-generation.

import torch
from diffusers import StableDiffusionPipeline
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5", torch_dtype=torch.float16
).to("cuda")

previews = []

def step_callback(pipe, step: int, timestep: int, callback_kwargs: dict):
    """Called after each denoising step. Decode latent for live preview."""
    latents = callback_kwargs["latents"]
    if step % 5 == 0:   # preview every 5 steps
        with torch.no_grad():
            decoded = pipe.vae.decode(latents / pipe.vae.config.scaling_factor).sample
        img = (decoded[0].permute(1,2,0).float().cpu().numpy() + 1) * 127.5
        previews.append(Image.fromarray(img.clip(0,255).astype(np.uint8)))
        print(f"Step {step}/{pipe.scheduler.timesteps.shape[0]}")
    return callback_kwargs   # must return the kwargs dict unchanged

result = pipe(
    prompt="a galaxy nebula, deep space photography, Hubble telescope style",
    num_inference_steps=30,
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    callback_on_step_end=step_callback,
    callback_on_step_end_tensor_inputs=["latents"],  # pass latents to callback
).images[0]

print(f"Generated {len(previews)} preview frames")
# previews[0] through previews[-1] show progressive denoising

ControlNet adds spatial conditioning to any SD pipeline by injecting a second U-Net (the ControlNet) whose residual outputs are added to the main U-Net's feature maps. The ControlNet is conditioned on a spatial control image — a Canny edge map, depth map, pose skeleton, or semantic segmentation. This allows precise spatial control over composition that text prompts alone cannot provide.

import torch
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
from diffusers import StableDiffusionControlNetPipeline, ControlNetModel
from diffusers.utils import load_image
import cv2

# Load Canny edge ControlNet
controlnet = ControlNetModel.from_pretrained(
    "lllyasviel/sd-controlnet-canny",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
)
pipe = StableDiffusionControlNetPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    controlnet=controlnet,
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")

# Prepare Canny edge control image
source = np.array(Image.open("building.png").resize((512, 512)))
edges  = cv2.Canny(source, threshold1=100, threshold2=200)
control_image = Image.fromarray(np.stack([edges, edges, edges], axis=-1))

# controlnet_conditioning_scale controls how strongly the spatial guide is followed
result = pipe(
    prompt="a futuristic glass skyscraper, sunny day, ultra detailed",
    negative_prompt="blurry, unrealistic, low quality",
    image=control_image,
    controlnet_conditioning_scale=0.8,   # 0.0 = ignore, 1.0 = strict
    guidance_scale=7.5,
    num_inference_steps=30,
    generator=torch.Generator("cuda").manual_seed(10),
).images[0]

Pipeline components are standard PyTorch modules and can be shared across pipelines to save VRAM. A text encoder and VAE loaded once can be injected into multiple downstream pipelines. Components passed by reference are not copied — all pipelines share the same weights in memory.

import torch
from diffusers import (
    StableDiffusionPipeline,
    StableDiffusionImg2ImgPipeline,
    StableDiffusionInpaintPipeline,
)

# Load the full pipeline once — all components are in memory
base_pipe = StableDiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-v1-5",
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")

# Derive specialised pipelines from the already-loaded components
img2img = StableDiffusionImg2ImgPipeline(
    vae=base_pipe.vae,
    text_encoder=base_pipe.text_encoder,
    tokenizer=base_pipe.tokenizer,
    unet=base_pipe.unet,
    scheduler=base_pipe.scheduler,
    safety_checker=base_pipe.safety_checker,
    feature_extractor=base_pipe.feature_extractor,
)

inpaint = StableDiffusionInpaintPipeline.from_pretrained(
    "runwayml/stable-diffusion-inpainting",
    vae=base_pipe.vae,           # share the VAE
    text_encoder=base_pipe.text_encoder,  # share text encoder
    tokenizer=base_pipe.tokenizer,
    torch_dtype=torch.float16,
).to("cuda")
# Only the inpainting U-Net (9-channel input) and scheduler are freshly loaded
# Total additional VRAM: ~0.86B params (inpainting U-Net) not the full model twice

Diffusers pipelines are component bundles — text encoder, VAE, U-Net, scheduler, and safety checker — built on a common base class with shared loading and saving utilities. Task-specific subclasses (txt2img, img2img, inpainting, ControlNet, SDXL) handle their own denoising loops and preprocessing while sharing the same underlying weights. The scheduler is the only component that can be hot-swapped without reloading — DPM++ 2M Karras at 20 steps is the practical quality/speed optimum for most use cases. Memory optimisations stack: attention slicing + VAE slicing + model CPU offload allows 512×512 generation on 4GB VRAM at the cost of 5× slower inference. Components passed by reference are shared across pipelines, enabling multi-task setups without duplicating weights in VRAM.
